meaning: n.热烈欢迎,大喝采,大受欢迎,欢呼
examples: When she walked onstage she was given a standing ovation.
Mr. Gould was given a standing ovation and loud cheers when he finished his speech.
They became civic heroes and received a tumultuous ovation on their appearance in New York City.
After that kind ovation, some might argue that Dimon may be ready for a new challenge.
He received a standing ovation from the crowd, and the game resumed after an 11-minute delay.
